info.title: Invoicing operator Iopole API
info.description:
## Iopole API

To use the Iopole APIs, you must have a **`clientId`** and a **`clientSecret`**.

You can obtain these credentials by one of the following methods:
- 📧 Contact the Iopole team at **support@iopole.com**
- 🧪 Connect to **[Iopole Labs](https://labs.iopole.io)** to create your own sandbox environment

### 📚 API Documentation
- 🧪 **Preproduction**: [https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/)
- 🚀 **Production**: [https://docs.iopole.com/](https://docs.iopole.com/)

### 🛠️ Support & Monitoring (production only)
- 📈 **System status**: [https://uptime.iopole.com](https://uptime.iopole.com)
  - View real-time platform availability
  - Subscribe to incident notifications
  - Receive alerts for planned maintenance periods
- 📝 **Release notes**: [https://release.iopole.com](https://release.iopole.com)

get.description: Search and retrieve invoices using filtering criteria, returning metadata, business data, and the latest status for each invoice.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
🔎 **Search query**
The query syntax and supported fields are documented here:
- 📘 [Search usage](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/docs/iopole-api/search)
- 📘 [Available search fields](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/docs/iopole-api/apiDescriptions/invoice/searchInvoiceByFilter)

> ✍️ **Examples**
> - Iopole invoice Id: `invoice.invoiceId:"3142b1eb-3ffc-4f51-9bc2-3cbd778a69c9"`
> - Multiple states: `invoice.state:"NOT_DELIVERED" OR invoice.state:"WRONG_ROUTING"`
> - Direction: `invoice.direction:"INBOUND"`
> - State + direction: `invoice.state:"NOT_DELIVERED" AND invoice.direction:"INBOUND"`

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description:
Controls optional sections included in the response.

**Supported values**:
- `businessData`: include invoice business data (buyer/seller, document number, amount)
- `lastStatusData`: include latest status information

Example: `businessData,lastStatusData`

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
⏭️ **Offset** : Starting position in the result set

**Default / Min / Max:** 0 / 0 / 1000
⚠️Large offset values may impact performance

**Example**
`offset=500&limit=100` → skips 500 records and returns the next 100.

schema.description:
⏭️ **Offset** : Starting position in the result set

**Default / Min / Max:** 0 / 0 / 1000
⚠️Large offset values may impact performance

**Example**
`offset=500&limit=100` → skips 500 records and returns the next 100.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
🔢 **Limit** : Maximum number of records returned

**Default / Min / Max:** 50 / 1 / 200

**Example:** `offset=0&limit=50`
Returns the first 50 records.

schema.description:

200.description: Invoices id list received from given criteria

direction.description: The direction the invoice was handled (Received/Emitted).

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

403.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request, but it refuses to authorize it.

get.description:
📄 **Invoice metadata**
Retrieve the **metadata and business data** associated with a given invoice:

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

streamId.description: The stream ID of the invoice.

date.description: {"valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","text":"Date of invoice creation in Iopole system"}

originalFormat.description: {"text":"Detected format when the invoice has been received"}

way.description: The way the invoice was handled (Received/Emitted).

invoiceId.description: {"valueDescription":"20 char","more":"BT-1 - Sequential number required by Article 226(2) of Directive 2006/112/EC [2], to uniquely identify the invoice. It can be based on one or more series, which may include alphanumeric characters.","text":"Invoice number"}

invoiceDate.description: {"valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","more":"BT-2 - Date on which the invoice was issued.","text":"Date of invoice"}

invoiceDueDate.description: {"valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","more":"BT-9 - The due date corresponds to the date when the net payment is due. For partial payments, it is the first net due date. The description for more complex payment terms is provided in BT-20.","text":"Date when the payment is due"}

anyOf.description: VAT is chargeable on invoice date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on delivery date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on payment date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on invoice date (Ubl)
VAT is chargeable on delivery date (Ubl)
VAT is chargeable on payment date (Ubl)

code.description:
{"text":"Code specifying the date on which VAT becomes chargeable for the Seller and the Buyer.","valueDescription":"One of the following values (CII or UBL binding): 5, 29, 72, 3, 35, 432","more":"BT-8 - VAT point date code.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

iopCode.description:
{"text":"IOPOLE VAT point date (platform enum).","valueDescription":"One of: 'UNKNOWN', 'INVOICE_DATE', 'DELIVERY_DATE', 'PAYMENT_DATE'","more":"IOPOLE extension - Alternative to BT-8 numeric code.
At least one of {code, iopCode} is required (both allowed).

Mapping to EN16931 code:
  INVOICE_DATE  -> CII: 5  | UBL: 3
  DELIVERY_DATE -> CII: 29 | UBL: 35
  PAYMENT_DATE  -> CII: 72 | UBL: 432
  UNKNOWN       -> not exported (or defaults depending on profile)
"}

taxPaymentOption.description:
{"text":"Code specifying the date on which VAT becomes chargeable for the Seller and the Buyer.","valueDescription":"One of the following values (CII or UBL binding): 5, 29, 72, 3, 35, 432","more":"BT-8 - VAT point date code.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

🎯 **Purpose – Acknowledgement**

Call this endpoint **after** the invoice has been **successfully integrated into the operator system**.
Once marked as seen, the invoice will **no longer be returned** by **`GET /v1/invoice/notSeen`**.

🔄 **PULL mode only**

This endpoint is **relevant ONLY when using PULL mode**.
In PUSH mode, received invoices are delivered via **webhooks** and this endpoint should not be used.

🎯 **Purpose – Invoice polling**

Retrieve the list of **invoice identifiers not yet marked as seen**.

it **must be explicitly marked as seen** by calling:
**`PUT /v1/invoice/{invoiceId}/markAsSeen`**

post.description:
🎯 **Purpose (Send invoice asynchronous)**

Send an invoice to a **remote platform**.
Supported invoice formats include **Factur-X (PDF)**, **UBL (XML)**, and **CII (XML)**.

🧾 **Participant requirements (important)**

- The **seller** referenced in the invoice **must belong to the operator internal directory**.
- The **buyer** must be **registered and reachable in the target network**.

🆔 **Invoice identifier (important)**

The identifier returned by this endpoint **must be stored** by the operator.
It is required to:
- send **outbound status updates**, and
- **reconcile inbound status notifications** related to this invoice.

🧩 **Invoice generation**

The operator may either:
- Generate compliant invoices manually by following the official specifications:
  - [**Peppol BIS 3**](https://docs.peppol.eu/poacc/billing/3.0/)
  - [**French domestic formats**](https://fnfe-mpe.org/ressources/)
- **Or** use the Iopole **invoice generation endpoints** to produce compliant documents from json format.

⏱️ **Asynchronous processing**

A successful response indicates that the invoice has been **accepted for processing**,
not that it has already been delivered to the recipient platform.

🧾 **Generate Factur-X invoice**

- Generate a **Factur-X invoice** from the **Iopole JSON** format.
- Output is a **PDF/A-3** document with an **embedded CII XML attachment**.
- Includes required **XMP metadata**.

After generation, The embedded CII is **validated** against the selected **flavor business rules**.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

Mapping to EN16931 code:

post.description:
🧾 **Generate CII invoice**

Generate a **CII invoice** from the **Iopole JSON** format.

After generation, the resulting invoice is **validated** against the selected **flavor business rules**.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: flavor
parameters.description: Invoice flavor

invoiceId.description: {"valueDescription":"20 char","more":"BT-1 - Sequential number required by Article 226(2) of Directive 2006/112/EC [2], to uniquely identify the invoice. It can be based on one or more series, which may include alphanumeric characters.","text":"Invoice number"}

invoiceDate.description: {"valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","more":"BT-2 - Date on which the invoice was issued.","text":"Date of invoice"}

invoiceDueDate.description: {"valueDescription":"YYYY-MM-DD","more":"BT-9 - The due date corresponds to the date when the net payment is due. For partial payments, it is the first net due date. The description for more complex payment terms is provided in BT-20.","text":"Date when the payment is due"}

anyOf.description: VAT is chargeable on invoice date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on delivery date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on payment date (Cii)
VAT is chargeable on invoice date (Ubl)
VAT is chargeable on delivery date (Ubl)
VAT is chargeable on payment date (Ubl)

code.description:
{"text":"Code specifying the date on which VAT becomes chargeable for the Seller and the Buyer.","valueDescription":"One of the following values (CII or UBL binding): 5, 29, 72, 3, 35, 432","more":"BT-8 - VAT point date code.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

iopCode.description:
{"text":"IOPOLE VAT point date (platform enum).","valueDescription":"One of: 'UNKNOWN', 'INVOICE_DATE', 'DELIVERY_DATE', 'PAYMENT_DATE'","more":"IOPOLE extension - Alternative to BT-8 numeric code.
At least one of {code, iopCode} is required (both allowed).

Mapping to EN16931 code:
  INVOICE_DATE  -> CII: 5  | UBL: 3
  DELIVERY_DATE -> CII: 29 | UBL: 35
  PAYMENT_DATE  -> CII: 72 | UBL: 432
  UNKNOWN       -> not exported (or defaults depending on profile)
"}

taxPaymentOption.description:
{"text":"Code specifying the date on which VAT becomes chargeable for the Seller and the Buyer.","valueDescription":"One of the following values (CII or UBL binding): 5, 29, 72, 3, 35, 432","more":"BT-8 - VAT point date code.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

post.description:
🧾 **Generate UBL invoice**

Generate an **UBL invoice** from the **Iopole JSON** format or from **CII format**.
After generation, the resulting invoice is **validated** against the selected **flavor business rules**.

📦 **Request content type**

- Use **`application/json`** when providing an **Iopole JSON** invoice
- Use **`application/xml`** when providing a **CII** invoice

🌍 **Peppol international invoicing**

For international Peppol invoicing, you must use the **`PEPPOL_BIS_3`** flavor.

📎 **Readable PDF attachment**

A human-readable **PDF visualisation** may be embedded inside the UBL using Json `attachments` section
- The UBL XML remains the **only authoritative source** for processing and compliance.
- The PDF is provided **for information only**.
- ⚠️ **Take care of size**: embedded files increase message size and may be rejected by Peppol Access Points or AS4 gateways. Keep PDFs **small and lightweight**

code.description:
{"text":"Code specifying the date on which VAT becomes chargeable for the Seller and the Buyer.","valueDescription":"One of the following values (CII or UBL binding): 5, 29, 72, 3, 35, 432","more":"BT-8 - VAT point date code.

Accepted values:
CII (UNTDID 2475):
  5   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  29  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  72  = VAT is chargeable on payment date

UBL (EN16931 UBL binding codes used by your implementation):
  3   = VAT is chargeable on invoice date
  35  = VAT is chargeable on delivery date
  432 = VAT is chargeable on payment date
"}

iopCode.description:
{"text":"IOPOLE VAT point date (platform enum).","valueDescription":"One of: 'UNKNOWN', 'INVOICE_DATE', 'DELIVERY_DATE', 'PAYMENT_DATE'","more":"IOPOLE extension - Alternative to BT-8 numeric code.
At least one of {code, iopCode} is required (both allowed).

Mapping to EN16931 code:
  INVOICE_DATE  -> CII: 5  | UBL: 3
  DELIVERY_DATE -> CII: 29 | UBL: 35
  PAYMENT_DATE  -> CII: 72 | UBL: 432
  UNKNOWN       -> not exported (or defaults depending on profile)
"}

get.description:
🎯 **Purpose (Invoice status history)**

Retrieve the **complete status history** for a given invoice, including all past state changes and timestamps.

This endpoint is intended for **consultation and audit purposes only** (e.g., troubleshooting, traceability, UI display).

⚠️ **Important – Status polling**

This endpoint **must NOT be used** as part of a **status polling process**.
To retrieve unread status updates, use the dedicated polling endpoint:
**`GET /v1/invoice/status/notSeen`**

🚦 **Rate limiting**

This endpoint is subject to a **very low rate limit**.
Excessive usage may result in **throttling or temporary blocking**.

get.description:
🎯 **Purpose – Status polling**

Retrieve **not yet seen status updates**, grouped by invoice.
Only **new or unread** status events are returned by this endpoint.

This endpoint is the **dedicated mechanism** for **status polling**.
It is designed to efficiently retrieve incremental updates without re-fetching full status history.

🔄 **PULL mode only**

This endpoint is **relevant ONLY when using PULL mode**.
In PUSH mode, status updates are delivered via **webhooks** and this endpoint should not be used.

✅ **Mark as seen (required)**

Once a status update has been **successfully integrated into the operator system**,
it **must be explicitly marked as seen** by calling:
**`PUT /v1/invoice/status/{statusId}/markAsSeen`**

🎯 **Purpose – Acknowledgement**

Call this endpoint **after** the status update has been **successfully integrated into the operator system**.
Once marked as seen, the status will **no longer be returned** by **`GET /v1/invoice/status/notSeen`**.

🔄 **PULL mode only**

This endpoint is **relevant ONLY when using PULL mode**.
In PUSH mode, status updates are delivered via **webhooks** and this endpoint should not be used.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: statusId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the status to be marked as seen.

get.description:
🔍🇫🇷 **French directory search (authoritative)**

Use this endpoint to **search and retrieve authoritative registration data** for participants registered in the **French directory** based on the provided criteria (e.g., SIREN, SIRET).

🎯 **Purpose**

This endpoint is an **authoritative source** for the French domestic network.
It allows clients to determine whether a business entity is registered and to retrieve the **current routing information** applicable in the French context.

🚚 **Invoice routing (France)**

The **invoice routing and delivery process in the French context can rely on the response of this endpoint**.
If a participant is not returned by this search, it should be considered **not reachable in the French domestic network**.

ℹ️ **Notes**

- Results reflect the **current state of the French directory** at the time of the request.

🧪 **Environment behavior**

- In **sandbox**, only a **limited test subset** of the French directory is integrated (approximately **20,000 test companies**).
- In **preproduction**, a **full version** of the French directory is integrated; however, the data **may be outdated**.
- In **production**, the **latest available version** of the French directory is integrated and **updated daily**.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
🔎 **Search query**

Use this parameter to define the **search expression** applied to the French directory.
The query syntax and supported fields are documented here:
- 📘 [Search usage](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/docs/iopole-api/search)
- 📘 [Available search fields](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/docs/iopole-api/apiDescriptions/directory/readDirectoryFrSearch)

> ✍️ **Examples**
> 
> - Search by SIREN: `siren:"123456789"`
> - Search by name: `name:"IOPOLE"`
> - Search by name with wildcard: `name:"IOP*"`

get.description:
🎯 **Purpose**

This endpoint allows you to **search the public Peppol Directory** to discover whether a participant is **listed on the Peppol network**, and to retrieve **high-level, publicly available registration information**.

ℹ️ **Notes**

- Providers are **NOT REQUIRED** to update the Peppol Directory; therefore, a participant may still be reachable on the network even if this endpoint returns no result.
- If the participant address is already known, it is **more accurate** to use the lookup endpoint `(/v1/directory/international/check)` to retrieve the **latest registration details**.

⚠️ **Important – Invoice delivery**

The **invoice delivery process must NOT rely on the response of this endpoint**.
This endpoint is intended for **discovery and informational purposes only** and does **not guarantee routability or delivery eligibility**.

🧪 **Environment behavior**

- This endpoint is **not available in sandbox mode**.
- In **preproduction**, search are performed against the [test Peppol directory](https://test-directory.peppol.eu/public).
- In **production**, search are performed against the [production Peppol directory](https://directory.peppol.eu/public).

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Enter the name, address, ID or any other keyword of the entity you are looking for.

**Example**

get.summary: Verify PEPPOL participant and retrieve routing information
get.description:
✅ **Peppol lookup (authoritative)**

Use this endpoint to **verify a specific participant** and retrieve the **latest registration details** from the Peppol network (e.g., identifiers, capabilities, endpoints).

🎯 **Purpose**

This endpoint is the **authoritative source** to determine whether a participant is registered and to retrieve the **current routing/endpoint information**.

🚚 **Invoice routing**

The invoice routing and delivery process **can rely on the response of this endpoint** to compute the delivery target.

ℹ️ **Notes**

- If the participant is not found, it should be considered **not reachable via the Peppol network**.
- This endpoint can be used to retrieve 🇫🇷 French participant addresses as well.

🧪 **Environment behavior**

- This endpoint is **not available in sandbox mode**.
- In **preproduction**, lookups are performed against the **Peppol preproduction (SMK)** environment.
- In **production**, lookups are performed against the **Peppol production (SML)** environment.

parameters.name: customer-id
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

parameters.name: scheme
parameters.description: Participant identifier scheme (e.g., 0208, 0225, 9908). [Peppol Allowed schemes](https://docs.peppol.eu/edelivery/codelists/)

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Participant identifier value (e.g., 123456789).

200.description: Returns true if given address exist, false otherwise
